name: god of fertility
shortDescription: In Norse mythology, the god of fertility is primarily associated with the Vanir god Freyr. He is responsible for prosperity, good harvests, and virility.
description: In Norse mythology, the primary god associated with fertility is Freyr, a member of the Vanir tribe of deities. He is not solely a god of fertility in the narrow sense, but rather a god of prosperity, virility, sacred kingship, sunshine, and fair weather, all of which contribute to the overall well-being and fertility of the land and its people. Freyr is often depicted with a prominent phallus, symbolizing his role in procreation and abundance. He is associated with peace and pleasure, and his blessings are sought for fruitful harvests and successful endeavors. Freyr's sister, Freyja, also plays a significant role in fertility, but she also embodies love, beauty, and war. While other gods and goddesses might influence specific aspects of fertility, Freyr is the central figure. Freyr's association with the Vanir, a tribe of deities known for their connection to nature and magic, further emphasizes his role as a bringer of life and abundance. He rides the golden boar Gullinbursti, a symbol of wealth and prosperity, and possesses the ship Skíðblaðnir, which can be folded up and carried in his pocket but can always have a fair wind when at sea. The lack of explicit and detailed myths solely focused on 'fertility rites' surrounding Freyr doesn't diminish his core function; his overall presence assures the vitality of the world. His eventual sacrifice of his sword to win the hand of the giantess Gerðr is a significant event that foreshadows the end of the world (Ragnarök), demonstrating the complex intertwining of fertility with other aspects of Norse cosmology.

sources.title: Gods and Myths of Northern Europe
sources.author: H.R. Ellis Davidson
